rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE NANCY MAZZA 1996 REVOCABLE INTER-VIVOS TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Demurrer And Demurrer To Petition For Order Authorizing And Directing Transfer Of Property From Surviving Spouse To Deceased Spouse'S Estate
rulings.Rulings: This tentative is issued by Judge Joseph M. Quinn. 1 of 2 Surviving Spouse Susan Hogeland's Request for Judicial Notice is GRANTED. (See Evid. Code 452, 453.) Notice is limited to the relevant facts properly subject to notice given the materials presented and does not, for example, extend to the truth of assertions in pleadings or transcripts. (See Richtek USA, Inc. v. UPI Semiconductor Corp. (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 651, 659-660.) Nancy Mazza is the decedent. Nancy, Thomas Mazza and James Mazza were siblings. Susan Hogeland was Nancy's spouse. Susan is the executor of Nancy's estate ("Estate"), which is governed by a will that provides for certain specific bequests with the remainder going to The Nancy Mazza 1996 Revocable Inter-Vivos Trust ("Trust"). Thomas and James are beneficiaries of the Trust. In this proceeding, Susan has filed a petition alleging that the Trust is in possession of community property held by Nancy at the time of her death and asking the court to transfer those assets out of the Trust and to her. Thomas and James have filed the instant Petition for Order Authorizing and Directing Transfer of Property from Surviving Spouse to Deceased Spouse's Estate ("Petition"). They allege that Susan is in possession of community property assets and to the extent those assets exceed the value of community property assets held by Nancy at the time of her death, the assets must be turned over to the Estate. Specifically, Thomas and James seek adjudication of "the community property rights of Nancy [] and Susan [] in the assets held by each of Nancy and Susan, individually and in trust, as of Nancy's death." In sum, the companion petitions seek to have Nancy (now her executor and the trustees of the Trust) identify the community property held by Nancy and have Susan identify the community property held by her so that half of community property is attributed to Nancy and held by the Estate or Trust and half attributed to Susan and held by her. Susan demurred to the Petition. Susan's demurrer is OVERRULED. The issue on demurrer is, taking the facts properly pleaded and properly noticed as true, does the Petition necessarily fail to state a claim for relief against the Susan for assets belonging to the Estate. (See Code Civ. Proc. 430.10(e); Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, 318.) "[I]f, on consideration of all facts stated, it appears that plaintiff is entitled to any relief against defendant, the complaint will be held good, though facts may not be clearly stated, or may be intermingled with statement of other facts irrelevant to cause of action shown, or though plaintiff may demand relief to which he is not entitled under facts alleged." ?(Augustine v. Trucco (1954) 124 Cal.App.2d 229, 236, quoting Matteson v. Wagoner (1905) 147 Cal. 739, 742.) Susan contends that the court has no jurisdiction over the subject of the cause of action alleged in the pleading. (See CCP 430.10, subd. (a).) The court has jurisdiction over the Trust, the Estate and property that should be in the possession of the Estate. Susan's contention that the court is without jurisdiction over an ERISA plan and real property in Mexico is beside the point. Also beside the point are the Petition's non-viable legal contentions. A demurrer must demonstrate no right to any relief under the cause of action. (See Augustine, supra, 124 Cal.App.2d at p. 236.) Here, there is one cause of action for transfer of community property, such as assets acquired with Susan's salary earned during the marriage, from Susan to the Estate. The allegations, if true, establish a right to at least some relief. Susan has not demonstrated that the Petition does not state facts sufficient to state a cause of action. (See CCP 430.10, subd. (e).) If, as alleged, Susan is in possession of community property, then a share thereof may well belong to the Estate.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE NANCY MAZZA 1996 REVOCABLE INTER-VIVOS TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Hearing On Demurrer To Petition For Order Authorizing And Directing Transfer Of Property From Surviving Spouse To Deceased Spouse'S Estate (Added For Posting Of The Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: This tentative is issued by Judge Joseph M. Quinn. 2 of 2 Nor has Susan demonstrated that the Petition is uncertain. (See CCP 430.10, subd. (f).) Liberally construed, the Petition gives fair notice of the allegations and claim for relief. It is worth noting that the court is sitting in equity. As for certainty, the Petition is on par with Susan's companion petition. The reality-acknowledged by the parties-is that Nancy (now her executor and the trustees of the Trust) knows best what community property Nancy held and Susan knows best what community property she holds and, therefore, both petitions will require sorting by way of detailed forensic accounting. Susan's arguments presented for the first time in the Reply Brief come too late and will not be considered. (See Bell v. H.F. Cox, Inc. (2012) 209 Cal.App.4th 62, 80, fn. 7.) Susan must file her response and/or substantive objections within ten calendar days of entry of this order. Petitioners shall prepare and lodge with the court clerk in Department 204 a hard-copy proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on March 25, 2021. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. The appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.
